Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: “ABAP AI is our Shared Treasure” - ABAP AI is on its way.
entry.content:
It’s time to talk about the announced ABAP AI capabilities, planned to be delivered with the 2502 release and beyond. ABAP AI is important to all SAP business software developers. It is our shared treasure that we hold in our hands. Let’s take a moment to elaborate a little while on this statement.  

ABAP AI is powerful

The new Joule copilot integrated into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, offers a full-fledged copilot experience and by that revolutionizes the way ABAP developers work. It provides AI support during the development process, significantly accelerating the creation and testing of custom ABAP applications. These groundbreaking developments have already been revealed and demonstrated at SAP TechEd 2024.

Imagine someone sitting next to you and guiding you, to write very good ABAP code. Not too bad, you might say. Now imagine that person complementing you and even starting to write code or to do unit tests for you. You can even discuss with this person and have them explain code you don’t understand.

Let's take a closer look at some of Joule's ABAP developer capabilities.

Joule for ABAP Code Prediction (Ghost Texting)

This feature helps you code. As you start typing your code, Ghost Text uses AI to propose the next lines of code. The difference between classic code completion already available in der development tools and this new feature is that ABAP AI actually understands the context and makes intelligent suggestions that are aligned with your coding objective. This is made possible by GenAI and SAPs trained ABAP model, which is designed to understand and predict ABAP coding patterns. This allows you to spend even more time solving complex problems and innovating.

ABAP AI Unit Agent

The ABAP AI Unit Agent is designed to assist you generate unit tests for an ABAP class. This ensures that your code is thoroughly tested. Traditionally, you would have to write unit tests manually, which is time-consuming and likely to be error-prone. With the ABAP AI Unit Agent, you can automate this process: You run the unit test for the ABAP class and check the code coverage option. The ABAP Unit Test analyzes the code coverage and identifies the methods that are not adequately covered. The ABAP AI Unit Agent adds to this the generation of unit tests.

ABAP and Core Data Services Code Explain

This feature makes ABAP, including ABAP Core Data Services (CDS), easier for developers to understand. When you select an ABAP class, CDS view, function module, a method or a code snippet, ABAP & CDS Explain analyzes the code and generates a human-readable explanation. This explanation includes a high-level overview of what the code does, as well as detailed explanations of each component. It is designed to understand ABAP coding patterns and conventions, so that it can provide accurate and useful explanations. By providing clear explanations of complex function modules, it helps developers improve their understanding of ABAP coding patterns, and best practices.

ABAP AI is attractive
SAPs ABAP AI SDK, powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM ), serves as an "AI toolbox" for ABAP developers. It allows  you to incorporate AI capabilities to your custom ABAP applications. The ABAP AI SDK integrates seamlessly with the SAP BTP GenAI Hub, enabling you to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and infuse AI capabilities into your custom applications. This SDK is also used internally for all ABAP-based AI solutions.

With the ABAP AI SDK, you can easily access and use the models and features provided by the Hub by calling released ABAP classes and interfaces directly in your application code. The ABAP AI SDK takes care of the rest, executing prompts and handling the complexities of integrating AI capabilities into ABAP. This seamless integration significantly simplifies the process of incorporating AI into business applications. If that is not attractive, what is?

ABAP AI is a treasure

Even more powerful than the ABAP AI developer use cases mentioned above is the ABAP custom code migration use case. Our shared goal is to help our customers and partners to transform their custom code and move into SAP’s cloud universe.

ABAP AI for Custom Code Migration will be a game changer in achieving this goal.

We will help you transform your custom code by accelerating the transformation of custom code. Whether it's the mandatory transformation of ABAP code when migrating from ECC to SAP S/4HANA or the subsequent transformation of ABAP code to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core.

ABAP AI support is provided for the migration of from the classic business suite to SAP S/4HANA and to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core. This will include explanations of the mandatory simplification items to be considered during your SAP S/4HANA transformation, as well as code suggestions for refactoring your classic ABAP code towards ABAP Cloud. The corresponding AI features will be integrated in the Custom Code Migration App as well as in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse.

Let us summarize: ABAP AI is powerful. ABAP AI is attractive. ABAP AI is a treasure.

 

Have a look at the ABAP AI roadmap for more information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cross-product/roadmap-info/genai

entry.title: Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAP Analytics Cloud), Controlled release in Q1 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

We’re excited to announce Analytical Insights in Joule, powered by SAP Analytics Cloud with the upcoming QRC1.2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud. This post provides an overview of the capability, its current scope, future directions, prerequisites, and release plans.

Analytics Insights in Joule – Overview and Functionality

What are Analytical Insights in Joule?

Today, Joule can be enabled in many SAP applications like SAP SuccessFactors, S/4HANA etc. In case you are not aware and want to get started with Joule, Refer Integrating Joule in SAP solutions.

If you have Joule already enabled in any of your SAP applications, then analytical capability in Joule, powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, allows business users to explore their data and gain insights into your business, in a natural, conversational experience. Using familiar business terms, simply ask your question, and Joule will instantly provide answers as charts and metrics.

Joule works with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud. This means that the administrator for Just Ask in the SAP Analytics Cloud determines what models, and therefore data, are available to in Joule once the analytical capability is enabled.

What is in the controlled release? (incl. restrictions)

In the first release of SAP Analytics Cloud and Joule integration, any end-user who has access to Joule and has a valid end-user in SAP Analytics Cloud tenant can access this capability in Joule.  Further, your analytical universe in SAP Analytics Cloud can only consist of Acquired/Imported SAC models specifically enabled for use in Just Ask.

With the first release, you will be able to realize the following benefits:

  • Ask analytical questions naturally in your language via Joule (which can soon be enabled in your cloud application, if not already is)
  • You don’t have to login to SAP Analytics Cloud to get analytical insights, instead get them from the context of your business application

Note the following restrictions exist during controlled release. We will try to lift them as soon as possible in the upcoming releases:

  • Joule itself cannot yet be enabled in SAP Analytics Cloud as opposed to other LOB Applications we listed above (for SAP Analytics Cloud this is work in progress and is planned for future releases)
  • Currently, only acquired (imported) models are supported. No live data models are supported.
  • Analytical Insights can only be metrices and charts.
  • The analytical capability is only supported when the Joule client interface is in U.S. English.
  • Google Chrome and New Microsoft Edge browsers are supported.

Infrastructure & Prerequisites

What are the prerequisites to access this capability?

Following are the set of pre-requisites for this capability to be available:

  • You should have access to Joule in one or more of SAP cloud applications
  • You should have SAP Analytics Cloud in your landscape
  • An end-user can access this capability only if has a valid end-user in SAP Analytics Cloud

Controlled release & Way forward

Who can participate in the Controlled Release?

You must be a customer of both SAP Analytics Cloud and any of SAP cloud application that has Joule enabled. Your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant must be running in Cloud Foundry, NEO landscape is not planned to be supported.

How can I sign up?

Registration is now open until 28th Feb 2025, and spots are limited! Don’t miss your chance to be part of this controlled release. Apply Here  

When can I expect general availability?

General availability is planned for QRC2.2025. Note that availability only applies to SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on Cloud Foundry.

Conclusion

We are looking forward to welcoming customers to this controlled release and to make this capability available to more and more customers in 2025. We shall deliver additional functionality unlocking more of the potential of this incredibly powerful integration.

entry.title: SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build: what a great experience!
entry.content:
At the end of last week I had a very stimulating trip to Berlin, where I participated in the SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build. It was an interesting event and I'm happy to share my takeaways with you.

Thursday November 27, 8:30 AM: I join my colleagues who arrived from Walldorf and Helsinki for a breakfast at our hotel, and we quickly head up to SAP Berlin office. Once there, together with the Berlin colleagues, we set up the event rooms we had booked for the keynote and then the hands-on workshop.

At 10.30, attendees start to arrive: we have the pleasure to welcome business users and developers from Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG), Bundesdruckerei, Stiftung Das Rauhe Haus, Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin as well as partners and a few SAP employees. All together, we engage discussions over a buffet of snacks before the event kicks off at 11.00 with our keynote, joined by many attendees on LinkedIn live and moderated by Esmeé.

Keynote and solution deep dive

Diana Roesner starts the keynote by introducing the Clean Core concept and explaining how SAP Build can be used to easily create side-by-side extensions to an SAP application. On this very important topic, I highly recommend you a new white paper - “Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” - and a crisp and easy-to consume learning journey - “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”.

We then dive into the different components of the SAP Build solution, with my close colleagues Kanishka presenting SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Code, and Yumnah describing SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Work Zone. Jesse gives a great demo of how SAP Build can help 🎅🏻 Santa Company 🎄 streamline order approvals by creating an extension directly from his SAP S/4HANA ERP using our Extensibility Wizard. Finally Hemant provides an insightful perspective on how SAP Build supports line-of-businesses, including finance, procurement, supply chain, customer experience, human resources, IT and cross-functional:

Following the presentations, our experts held a Q&A session with the audience, answering the questions raised on-site as well as in the online chat:

We received very positive feedback from the audience on this session, which you can watch as a replay.

You can also download the slides of this keynote.

Hands-on Workshop

After a short break, we moved to another room for the hands-on workshop. In 3 hours, our local participants had the opportunity to create a full-stack application to improve customer loyalty. The purpose of the application is to allow customers to redeem points from their purchases. To do this, the application needs to access customer, purchase and product data from SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, workshop participants experienced using SAP Build Code to create a back-end CAP service with Joule AI copilot, highlighting SAP's generative AI power for development. The customer loyalty application is connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as an extension to the ERP, so the participants learned how to use a BTP destination. And they used SAP Build Apps to create a mobile app, showcasing our modern low-code/no-code development tool.

This is the part of the event I was involved in, and I have to say it was great and rewarding to see our participants amazed at how Joule helped them create data entities, sample data and JavaScript code from simple prompts.  They really experienced the power of Generative AI that we showcased at SAP TechEd a few weeks ago!

If you would like to do this hands-on exercise and experience Joule by yourself, it’s very easy!

Indeed, it is available here in our Basic Trial environment, where you will have immediate access to all the tools needed: SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot.

Christmas Market at AGILITA’s

At 16:00 we jumped into a bus who drove us to the AGILITA office in Berlin. There, AGILITA Christmas Market brought everyone together to reflect and share ideas in a festive atmosphere.

Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience for AGILITA, presented SAP AppHaus Workshops: SAP and AGILITA are helping customers find the right path to digitalization with design and architecture thinking workshops. Customers discover how to integrate Al and innovation for their own use cases, and how to effectively improve their business processes.

We also had the opportunity to attend very interesting demos of applications developed by the AGILITA team with SAP Build, showing how tasks like recording time or logging work can happen in just 3 clicks:  

AGIL AI.TimeManager  - Optimize your time and activity recording with AGIL Time Manager, the flexible and user- friendly solution for efficient time management and seamless integration in real-time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.TimeManager in SAP Store.

 

AGIL Al.Sales  - Boost sales performance with the power of voice and Al synergy. Effortlessly dictate orders and meeting notes while on the go - advanced generative Al transcribes, analyzes, and seamlessly updates your systems in real time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.Sales in SAP Store.

 Discover more AGILITA’s applications and extensions in SAP Store.

Up to you now!

You too can take advantage of the content we have prepared for this event. And it's very simple!

  1. Watch the keynote replay 🎞  ️
  2. Download the presentation 👍🏻  
  3. Do the hands-on exercise 👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓

 

Personal note

To end this blog post on a more personal note, I would like to say that I had 2 very energizing days in Berlin. I only work 3 days a week, Monday-Wednesday, but when I was asked if I could attend this event in Berlin on a Thursday, I didn't hesitate for a second: as I work 100% from home in Lyon, it was a great opportunity to meet up with colleagues from Berlin and Walldorf. The day after the event, we took time to reflect on what we did in 2024 and what we would like to do in 2025. This trip to Berlin was also a nice opportunity to meet great people from our valued partner AGILITA.

And when I was on my way back to Lyon on Friday evening, what caught my eye in the Berlin airport terminal?

On a big screen, an ad from SAP promoting our magical trifecta of Process + Data + AI 😊

entry.title: Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
entry.content:
Just recently, Joule - our generative AI copilot turned 1!
In this one year, Joule has revolutionized the way how users interact with their business solutions, be it HR, Sales, Procurement or IT – but there was one significant piece missing: mobile access.

With our latest releases of SAP Mobile Start, you can now use Joule while being mobile. Instead of searching for the right app, opening it, and then looking for the info, you can now ask Joule in natural language.

While our teams add more and more business content/ scenarios and functions to Joule, we also plan to embed Joule into further mobile apps, like SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Service and Asset Manager.

But now, let´s have a look at how Joule within SAP Mobile Start helps you work faster, get smarter insights when you need them, and interact more smoothly with your business systems while being mobile.

The Joule mobile client is embedded within the SAP Mobile Start header bar and can be opened with a single tap from any main screen. It provides the same main functionality and content as the desktop client if the content is enabled for mobile usage.

Screenshot: SAP Mobile Start with embedded Joule

After opening the Joule client, you just enter your commands in natural language and Joule provides you with the respective answer.

Let´s have a look at three concrete examples:
Imagine you are in a meeting and need to check on your sales orders that have not been delivered so far. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just ask “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed” and Joule will provide you the respective list.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example open orders (SAP S/4HANA)

Or you are a consultant who is new to the role, and a customer asks you how to customize a sales order in SAP S/4HANA. With Joule in SAP Mobile Start, you just pull out your phone and ask Joule, and you will get immediately the right SAP Help pages listed.

 Screenshot: Joule – Example documentation (SAP Help)

Finally, imagine you have a new team member, Maria, who has just joined from another team, and you want to quickly check when she has initially joined the company. Just ask Joule.

 
Screenshot: Joule – Example hiring date (SAP SuccessFactors)

Joule also allows you to navigate to other apps to get further details. Those could be web apps or native apps like the native SAP SuccessFactors app. So, if you want to learn more about Maria and her career so far, you just tap the “Profile” button and Joule will open Maria´s profile in the native SAP SuccessFactors app on your phone, with all the information about your new team member.

 Screenshot: Example native app integration

To initiate a Joule conversation, you're not limited to type in the request. You can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which even works better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Joule truly changes the way you interact with your business systems – it becomes much more natural. You can articulate your business needs in natural language and Joule responds by swiftly providing seamless access to your enablement content what will help you to execute your tasks faster and in a far more convenient way.

As of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; but more and more Joule business skills and scenarios are added. We are also working on adding some valuable mobile-specific Joule capabilities, so stay tuned for more.

If you have enabled Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, or SAP Start, the mobile Joule client will automatically be available in SAP Mobile Start. No additional steps are needed.

Do not miss our brand new Joule in SAP Mobile Start video: “Bring Joule Everywhere! SAP's AI Copilot is Now Available on Mobile”. 

Besides bringing Joule to mobile, we have added more helpful features to further enhance your experience with SAP Mobile Start, like Personalization of the Start Screen. With the new release, you can easily move the Start Screen sections such as Insights or News up or down as they work best for you – and hide sections if you do not need them. As always, it is super easy and intuitive and will boost your productivity as you can adjust the Start Screen now, based on your preferences.

 Screenshot: SAP Start Screen personalization

Finally, we have enhanced the Apps and To-Dos Screen. Now you can expand and collapse your spaces and pages section on the app screen, and we have provided an enhanced filtering experience on the To-Do screen that allows you to set multiple filters.

Screenshot: To-Dos Screen with filtering option

I hope you will enjoy those new features in SAP Mobile Start.

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.
SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

entry.title: Infusing AI into Journal Entry Process - Beyond Automation
entry.content:
Background:

During critical periods such as month-end, quarter-end, or year-end close, organizations often face a high volume of manual journal entries being posted into their S/4  systems. These entries can sometimes contain errors, especially when they are entered by various users who might not have a consistent understanding of the required formats, values, or related documentation.

Currently, these manual entries are posted directly into the system without prior checks, leading to potential discrepancies, accounting errors, or compliance violations. These errors could include incorrect amounts, incorrect GL codes, misaligned descriptions, missing or inaccurate supporting documentation, and others. To mitigate this risk, a more streamlined and controlled process is needed where the journal entries are parked first, reviewed for validation, and then posted after being confirmed as correct.

Key Issues:

  1. Manual Journal Entry Errors: These include issues such as:
    • Incorrect amounts posted for a particular entry type (e.g., a cost that is typically $5,000 being entered as $500).
    • Incorrect General Ledger (GL) codes or descriptions (e.g., the GL code does not match the standard or policy documentation).
    • Missing or incorrect attachment references (e.g., an invoice or supporting document is either not attached or is attached to the wrong entry).
    • Missing or incomplete information (e.g., missing approval, insufficient details for audit).
  2. Lack of Validation Prior to Posting: Since the journal entries are directly posted into the system, there is no opportunity to detect these errors before they are committed to the books, leading to potential audit issues, compliance risks, and manual corrections that are costly and time-consuming.

Solution Objective: To streamline this process, the goal is to implement a system where all manual journal entries are first "parked" (held temporarily) in the system, rather than posted immediately. During this parked state, the entries can undergo a series of automated checks or validations, leveraging historical data, company policy documents, and attachments to ensure accuracy and compliance. The system can then generate alerts or notifications to flag any anomalies, providing users with the opportunity to correct them before final posting.

Key Components: A Symphony of Technology

SAP BTP AI Services

Leverage pre-built AI models for document analysis, text extraction, and machine learning. These services enable intelligent data processing and automation.

S/4HANA

The core ERP system provides access to real-time financial data, enabling accurate and efficient journal entry creation and posting.

Data Pipelines

Securely transport data between BTP AI and S/4HANA, ensuring smooth and reliable integration of processed data for journal entries.

AI-Enabled Validation System:

An AI-enabled solution would help in detecting these anomalies by analyzing historical data, policy documents, and attachment details. The AI system would focus on the following validation areas:

  1. Amount Validation:
    • Historical Amount Comparison: AI can learn typical amounts for certain types of journal entries based on historical data. For example, if a specific type of entry (e.g., payroll) typically has an amount in the range of $4,500 to $5,500, the system can flag entries that are too high or too low.
    • Amount Consistency: Cross-checking the amounts with similar entries from previous periods or other business units to ensure consistency.
  2. GL Code Validation:
    • GL Code Matching: Ensuring that the correct GL code is used based on the entry type. If an entry for "supplies" is posted under a GL code used for "payroll", it can be flagged.
    • GL Code Description Match: The AI can validate that the description associated with the GL code aligns with the standard naming convention or description used in the policy documents.
    • Cross-checking with Financial Policies: Validation against internal financial policies or specific rules regarding what types of entries can use which GL codes.
  3. Description Validation:
    • Attachment Match: The AI can compare the GL description with the content of the attached document to ensure they are consistent. For example, if the entry refers to "Consulting Fees" but the attachment shows "Travel Expenses", the system can flag this discrepancy.
    • Standard Description Check: Ensuring that entries match the standard descriptions specified in internal accounting guidelines or corporate documentation.
  4. Attachment Validation:
    • Document Existence Check: Ensuring that required documents (e.g., invoices, contracts, receipts) are attached. If they are missing, an alert is generated.
    • Attachment Type Matching: The system can check whether the correct type of attachment is associated with the entry. For example, if the entry refers to a "Vendor Payment," the attached document can be an invoice or a vendor agreement.
    • Document Consistency: Comparing the details in the attachment (e.g., amounts, dates) with the amounts or other details in the journal entry to verify consistency.
  5. Approval Workflow Validation:
    • Approval Matching: Ensuring that all journal entries have been approved according to the organization’s internal controls. The AI can validate whether the appropriate manager or department head has provided the necessary approval for the entry type.
    • Approval Policy Check: Cross-checking entries against approval thresholds defined by the organization (e.g., entries above $10,000 must have senior management approval).
  6. Data Completeness:
    • Missing Information: Identifying any missing or incomplete fields in the journal entry. For example, ensuring that the "reason for entry" field or "cost center" field is populated.
    • Consistency with Historical Data: Cross-checking the current entry against similar entries in historical data to ensure that the same fields are populated appropriately.
  7. Audit & Compliance Validation:
    • Regulatory Compliance: Validating that the journal entry complies with relevant accounting regulations (e.g., GAAP, IFRS). The AI system can cross-check entries for common non-compliance patterns.
    • Audit Trail: Ensuring that there is a clear and complete audit trail for all parked journal entries, including details on who made the entry, when it was parked, and who approved it.
 

 

 

Solution Approach

  1. AI and Machine Learning Models:
    • Use machine learning algorithms that can be trained on historical data, previous journal entries, and standard accounting practices to recognize patterns of typical errors and anomalies.
    • The system can apply supervised learning techniques, using labeled data (e.g., previous correct and incorrect journal entries), to improve accuracy over time.
  2. Natural Language Processing (NLP):
    • To validate descriptions, attachment content, and other free-form text fields, NLP techniques can be applied to identify mismatches between the journal entry and the attached documentation.
  3. Integration with S/4  Systems:
    • The solution can seamlessly integrate with existing S/4  or accounting systems to "park" the journal entries and raise alerts automatically when discrepancies are found.
  4. Automated Alerts and Notifications:
    • Once anomalies are detected, the system can send automated alerts to relevant personnel (accountants, managers, auditors) with clear descriptions of the issue and suggested corrections.

Key Benefits

  • Improved Accuracy: By automating the validation of manual entries before posting, the AI system can significantly reduce the likelihood of errors.
  • Cost and Time Savings: Automating the review and validation process saves time and money that would otherwise be spent on manual corrections and audits.
  • Compliance and Risk Mitigation: Ensures that all entries comply with financial regulations and internal policies, reducing the risk of non-compliance and potential penalties.
  • Audit Readiness: The system maintains a comprehensive log of all validation checks, providing a clear audit trail for future reviews.

Type of Journal Entry

Description

S/4HANA AI Automation

Standard Entries

Monthly accruals for expenses like utilities or salaries.

S/4HANA uses AI to predict and automate recurring standard entries based on historical data, improving efficiency and accuracy.

Recurring Entries

Lease payments or subscription fees that occur regularly.

AI algorithms in S/4HANA identify patterns and automate the setup and posting of recurring entries, reducing manual intervention.

Adjusting Entries

Year-end adjustments for prepaid expenses or inventory changes.

S/4HANA's AI capabilities assist in suggesting necessary adjustments and automate postings based on predefined rules and criteria.

Accrual Entries

Accruing unpaid invoices for services received but not yet billed.

AI in S/4HANA predicts and accrues expenses and revenues, ensuring timely recognition and minimal manual oversight.

Reclassification Entries

Reclassifying expenses from one department to another.

AI tools help automate reclassifications by analyzing transaction data and suggesting necessary reallocations.

Tax-related Entries

Journal entries related to tax provisions or deferred tax liabilities.

S/4HANA utilizes AI to ensure tax compliance by automating complex calculations and adjustment entries according to tax laws.

Adjustments for Errors

Correcting a previously recorded entry due to misclassification or numerical error.

AI-driven error detection and correction features in S/4HANA help identify discrepancies and propose corrections efficiently.

 

In conclusion, implementing an AI-powered validation system for parked manual journal entries will streamline the accounting process, reduce errors, ensure compliance, and save significant time and effort during month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing periods.

Srinath Ganesan

entry.title: On-Premise AI Excellence: How SAP HANA EE and Skybuffer AI Deliver
entry.content:
All right, AI stories are nice and interesting; however, as SAP posted this week, “Today’s geopolitical environment is defined by uncertainty.” This is moving us toward the necessity of controlling innovative AI solutions in disconnected-from-Internet cases.

Can an Enterprise-Level AI Solution Run In Your Datacenter?
Of course, no problem at all! Let’s think a bit—there is an awesome SAP HANA Enterprise Edition available that has an embedded applications layer, and, in fact, this is a Cloud Foundry solution with all necessary enterprise security mechanisms, all delivered by SAP.

Fig. 1 Skybuffer AI Solution Architecture 

Great! So, we have the following on-premise ingredients:

  • SAP Cloud Foundry Cockpit
  • SAP HANA Database
  • SAP Applications Runtime
  • SAP Job Scheduling

These ingredients allow us to develop SAP Fiori applications using XSA technology directly on the SAP HANA EE application layer.

What is missing?
In a general sense, a few things are missing in SAP HANA EE on-premise to act as your enterprise AI on-premise platform:

  • SAP HANA Vector Engine for document information processing (RAG scenarios)
  • SAP AI Core
  • Action Server (no-code SAP Fiori applications allowing AI modeling)

Is the on-premise gap closed?
Yes! Starting in September 2024, our Skybuffer AI partner solution, developed on SAP technologies, successfully passed the pilot stage and is now available in the SAP Store in both versions:

  • SAP BTP Deployment
  • SAP HANA EE (on-premise) Deployment

We have brought the following missing parts to SAP HANA EE on-premise:

  • SAP HANA Partner Vector Engine
  • AI Core (de facto your preferred on-premise LLM model, for example, Llama 3.1 8B, running inside the SAP Applications Layer in Docker)
  • A set of SAP Fiori applications for no-code AI modeling

 Fig. 2 Skybuffer AI Modeling Dashboard

As a result, you get a ready-to-use platform that integrates Generative AI, RAG, and Conversational AI, allowing businesses to automate tasks quickly and efficiently. It saves costs, reduces development complexity, and accelerates time to value. Everything is on-premise on SAP HANA EE (which you can host even under the table 🙂 ).

Cherry on the cake!
Skybuffer AI is out-of-the-box connected to SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA on-premise systems. We have already developed approximately 400 different integrated business scenarios that allow you to use secure AI assistants connected to your SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA via single sign-on. Yes, another magic—no need to adjust roles and profiles for your users; they get in chat exactly the same information they are authorized to see in the SAP system.

Fig. 3 SAP ECC or SAP S4HANA in Microsoft Teams Channel

What channels are covered?
Communication can be done through many channels, the most popular are:

  • Microsoft Teams
  • Web Chat component (can be embedded in any web page)
  • Zoom
  • SAP Fiori Web Chat

Everything is just a configuration on the Skybuffer AI platform (on your premises)—no need for Python, Node.js, or any other type of development.

What about AI Agents?
Sure, those nice 24/7 employees are also here! Any AI model from Skybuffer AI can be scheduled to execute monitoring, document processing, or any other task. Each time an AI Agent runs, you see the execution results in the conversation log because the AI Agent uses generative AI to explain what has been done and tag the log.

 Fig. 4 AI Agent Generative Log

You can chat with the AI Agent anytime to check the heartbeat of the process. Is it not magic?

Fig. 5 AI Agents Status Check

What about RAG document processing?
Here, we not only supply an RAG solution that is ready to consume without any single line of code. Skybuffer AI also has an RAG Document Viewer that allows your employees to see the documents currently accessible by RAG, check document content, and upload new documents directly from the desktop.

 Fig. 6 Skybuffer AI - RAG Document Management

All documents have metadata parameters, so you can classify documents using 48 out-of-the-box string, numbering, and date-type parameters. What you need to do is define your aliases for parameters, so the RAG Fiori application will speak your company’s terms.

What about Joule?
Let’s wait for Q1 2025. Once SAP makes Joule Build available for SAP Partners, we will connect the Skybuffer AI platform to be available via the Joule communication channel as well. In other words, we complement Joule by bringing Skybuffer AI skills directly into Joule’s embedded AI skills.

Interesting and exciting? Agree!

What do you need to run enterprise-level AI on-premise?
Only SAP HANA, which you most probably already have!

Fig. 7 Skybuffer AI - Action Server for no-code AI Modeling

Performance?
Hmmm, that is something really cool. Old, nice on-premise performance, finally 🙂
